questions.text: What historical prediction is made in the opening of Surah Ar-Rum?
questions.answer: The victory of the Romans over the Persians within a few years (3 to 9 years) after their initial defeat.

questions.text: Why would the believers rejoice at the Roman victory?
questions.answer: Because it was a victory for the People of the Book (Christians) over the fire-worshippers (Persians), signifying the help of Allah.

questions.text: How do people generally view the life of this world (Verse 7)?
questions.answer: They know only the outward appearance of the worldly life, but are heedless of the Hereafter.

questions.text: What is the 'sign' mentioned regarding the creation of spouses (Verse 21)?
questions.answer: That He created for you from yourselves mates that you may find tranquility in them; and He placed between you affection and mercy.

questions.text: What are two signs of Allah mentioned in Verse 22?
questions.answer: The creation of the heavens and the earth, and the diversity of languages and colors.

questions.text: What is the sign mentioned regarding sleep (Verse 23)?
questions.answer: Your sleep by night and day and your seeking of His bounty.

questions.text: To whom does everything in the heavens and earth belong?
questions.answer: To Allah; all are devoutly obedient to Him.

questions.text: What is the 'Fitra' of Allah mentioned in Verse 30?
questions.answer: The natural inclination/disposition upon which Allah has created all mankind; there is no change in the creation of Allah.

questions.text: What is the ruling on Riba (interest/usury) in this Surah?
questions.answer: Whatever you give in interest to increase within the wealth of people will not increase with Allah.

questions.text: What is the reward for giving Zakat out of a desire for Allah's countenance?
questions.answer: Those are the ones who will have their reward multiplied.

questions.text: What causes corruption (Fasad) to appear throughout the land and sea?
questions.answer: What the hands of people have earned (their sins and disobedience).

questions.text: What is the purpose of Allah allowing some of the consequences of people's actions to reach them?
questions.answer: So that they may taste part of what they have done and perhaps they will return (to the right path).

questions.text: How does Allah describe the winds in Verse 46?
questions.answer: As heralds of glad tidings, to give you a taste of His mercy, and to make the ships sail by His command.

questions.text: To whom did Allah send Messengers before Muhammad (SAW)?
questions.answer: To their own people with clear proofs.

questions.text: How does Allah describe the revival of the dead in the context of nature?
questions.answer: He gives life to the earth after its death; indeed, that same Allah will give life to the dead.

questions.text: Can the Prophet make the dead hear or the deaf hear the call (Verse 52)?
questions.answer: No, especially when they turn their backs and flee.

questions.text: What is the sequence of human strength and weakness described in Verse 54?
questions.answer: Created in weakness, then given strength, then after strength, weakness and white hair (old age).

questions.text: What will the criminals swear on the Day of Judgment regarding the length of their stay?
questions.answer: They will swear they remained but an hour.

questions.text: What is the final instruction to the Prophet in this Surah?
questions.answer: To be patient, for the promise of Allah is truth, and not to let those who have no certainty discourage him.

questions.text: What does Allah say about those who 'split up their religion and became sects'?
questions.answer: Each faction rejoices in what it has.
